BUCAEFi

Roeent R. Welr.rxcrol.r

i

John wallace, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Politlcal Practices
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, Cal-ifornia
Re: Citv of Marina, Your File No. I-90-249
Dear Mr. Wallace:
Enclosed are supplementary rnaterials which you requested
concerning the question of whether the city 
of Marina has
"jurisdiction[ 
over the portion of Fort ord within its boundaries.
First is a letter from the staff Judge Advocate of Fort ord
in which he explains why the FederaL covernment has exclusive
Iegislative jurisdiction over the Military Reservation (Exhibit A).
His conclusion is based on the case la--w cited, but it is also
supported by 10 United States Code Section 2693 (Exhibit B), which
is the statutory basis for the relinquishment of legislative
jurisdiction over Federal lands to a state. this office has been
unabLe to locate any record of such relinquishnent of legislative
jurisdiction over Fort Ord and it must therefore be presurned that
the nere act of annexation did not change the powers of the various
governmental agencj.es but sirnply transferred those powers held by
the State to the City (Howard v. Conmissioners (1953) 344 U.S.
624).
AR (Army Regulation) 4O5-2O provides a definition 
and
discussion of the term t'Legislative Jurisdictiontt as wel-L as
"Exclusive Legislative Jurisdiction" (Exhibit C). 
A review of
these terms shows clearly that the Federal covernment has al-I of
the authority of the State which has not been reserved to the
State, to enact, execute and enforce general legislation in that
area. In 23 Op. A.c, Ca1. 14 (1954), the Attorney ceneral reviewed
the jurisdiction issue as it specifically related to Fort Ord and
concluded that the United States had exclusive jurisdiction 
over
the area. Further review of the statutes cited in that opinion,
Exhibit D, indicates that the State has reserved unto itself 
the
polrer to tax and to serve civil and criminal process, thus implying
that these are the onlv powers which are so reserved.


AR 405-25 (Exhibit E) further reinforces the continued and
substantial extent of Federal- jurisdiction 
after annexation by
stating 
rtAnnexation does not alter 
the existing 
Federal
jurisdiction over Army controlled land and does not result in any
interference with official 
Army activities 
or functions because
these are protected by Federal constitutj-onal immunity.'r
Hopefully these materials will assist you in your review of
this extremely important natter and hrill a11ow you to conclude that
the position of the City of Marina in this regard j.s correct. If
you have any questions or need any further infornation, please feel
free to contact this office.
Thank you for your continued courtesy and assistance in this
matter .
Very truly yours,
Assistant city Attorney
City of Marina

August 7, 1990
Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq.,
General counsel
Fair Politica1 Practices cornmission
LegaI Division
428 J street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95815
Re: City of Marina Request
Your File No. I-9 0-2 4 9
Dear Ms. Donovan:
for Reconsideration oi Inierpretation
The City of Marina has reviewed the response of your office dated
May 7, 1990, to the City Attorneyis letter of tlarch 20, 1990
requesting advice. Because of the severe inpact of the conclusion
that Regulation 18703.1 is not available for the City of Marina.
I have been authorized and directed by the City Council- to present
additional facts to the Comrnission in order to show that the City
does not have "jurisdictiont' over either the area or population of
the City, which is contained within Fort Ord and to take the
necessary actions to nake Regulation 18703.1 availabte to the City.
ft rrould appear that the Comrnissionrs conclusion is based on the
definition of jurisdiction found on page 2 of the advice letter and
the statement innediately following rrunder your facts, while the
city council's authority with respect to the area in question is
linited in conparision vith the council's authority over the rest
of the citv. the council, nevertheless, has some authority and
official 
duties qrith respect to the regionrr. This statenent 1s
contrary to the facts as was pointed out in the city Attorneyrs
letter of March 20, L990, which stated rtthe City has absolutely no
control over the population and territory 
found on Fort Ordrr and
trthe area and the population residing within it are not subject to
any of the laws of the City of Marinar.. The fact that persons
residing in the area vote in city elections does not support a
contention that the city has authority over the population but is
rather a quirk of the lan, Those voters residing on Fort ord have
the right to vote for the City Council but the City Council has no
porrer over those voters.


Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq. 
August 7, l99o
Page Two
The difficulty 
of not having the public generally exception apply
to the city of Marina was vividly illustrated by a recent neeting
of the Planning Commission. on July 23, L99o, the commission was
scheduled to hear a natter concerning a major renovation of a smal-l-
shopping center in the City. 
The commission consists of seven
members with a quorum being five. At that neeting tlro members were
absent and four were disqualified due to living within 2,500 feet
of the property under consj,deration. A quorum could not be
convened because under Regulation 18701 only two Cornmj,ss j,oners
could be seLected by Iot 
meaning only a total 
of three
Commissioners would be present. 
As a result the action was
required to be continued due to lack of a quorum. The dif f icuJ,ty
then arose as to when the matter could be considered because one of
the absent members e/as on record as stating he arould nost likeLy be
absent from commission meetings for two months. Even assuming that
Commissioner attended the next rneeting, there is no assurance that
another, non-disqual ified member would not be absent. In short, if
any one of the three non-disqualif ied mernbers is absent, the natter
could not be considered. Thi-s situation is not fair to the
developer nor fair to the Commissioners $rho must rnake their work
and recreation plan fit a schedute which is unpredictable and out
of their control in order to participate in a frequently thankless
job of sitting on the Planning Commission.
The above situation is not unusual in this city and has, in fact,
occurred on other occasions. Both the City council and the
Planning Commission have, because of the srnalI area which
enconpasses where elected and appointed officials 
reside,
encountered sinilar 
probLens of disqua I ification 
due to the
proxinity of their members' interests in real property to a
proj ect.
The City is not asking that this exception be expanded. Rather,
the City is asking that the Commission look again at what the terrn
"jurisdiction', 
means when the boundaries of the City do not
coincide with the control and authority of the City, 
Such an
examination of the facts regarding the city 
of 
Marinars
tt jurisdictionrr over territory within Fort Ord will reveal that the
city has absolutely no control over that territory. 
I am sure that
any inquiry to the Commanding General of Fort Ord, the Department
of the Army or any appropriate office of the Federal Government
would show that it was never intended that a City would have any
rrright to exercise official 
authoritytt over federal territory
annexed to a city as has occurred here.


Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq. 
August 7, L99O
Page Three
The City requests that the Commission reconsider its interpretation
of the term "jurj,sdiction" as it applies to the applicability of
Regulation 18703.1 or in the alternative adopt new language making
the public generally exception applicable to cities which exceed
the population and/or area linits of Regulation 18703,1 so1e1y due
to annexation of federal property.
The City would welcome the opportunity to discuss this natter
further with the Commission and/or the Chairnan. To that end the
City requests that a meeting be arranged in order to allo!, a
delegation from the City to present further information and anslrer
questions at the earliest possible date.
I look forward to hearing from your office as soon as possible. If
you have any questions on this natter, please feel free to contact
either the City Manager, John Longley, at (408) 384-3715 or the
City Attorney, Robert WeIIington, (408) 373-8733.
Very Truly yours,
Mayor
GJT/nh

Assenblynan San Farr
